title: KDP LAUNCH EXPERT (Full-Stack Publishing & Growth)
description:
Are you an expert at navigating Amazon KDP? Do you know how to take a manuscript and turn it into a bestseller through strategic category placement and management?



We are expanding our brand into the publishing space and need an expert to assist with this expansion on Amazon KDP for digital and print.



Key Responsibilities:



• Launch & SEO: Develop and execute a strategy to maximize organic ranking. Assist with keyword research (7-backend slots), SEO titles, and subtitle optimization using tools like BookBeam, Helium 10, or others.

• Category Strategy: Research and select high-relevance, low-competition categories as a way to help secure the "Bestseller" badges.

• A+ Content & Listings: Design high-converting A+ Content (From the Publisher section), Look Inside optimization, and compelling sales copy that triggers conversions.

• Review Generation: Implement any relevant strategies and automations to build early social proof.

• AMS & Attribution: Manage Amazon Marketing Services (Auto, Keyword, and Product Targeting ads). Use Amazon Attribution to track and reward traffic driven from our TikTok and Shopify channels.



Requirements:



• Proven KDP Launches: A portfolio showing books you have successfully ranked in the Top 100 of their respective categories.

• Ads Mastery: Deep knowledge of AMS (Amazon Advertising) specifically for books.

• Design & Layout: Proficiency in Canva/Photoshop for A+ content.

• Communication: Excellent command of written English.



Additional Information:

• Proof of Work: You must share active KDP links you’ve managed and screenshots of KDP Reports/Ads Manager to verify your role.

• Long-term opportunity to increase your hours, increase your pay, and become a bigger part of the team long-term.



To Apply, You Must:

1) Apply here on OLJ using *5* Apply Points.

2) Fill out this form: 
 

Applications that do not complete items 1 & 2 will not be considered.



If you move to the next steps, you will be asked to participate in a technical interview. During this live session, you will learn about our brand expansion to Amazon KDP and call out exactly how you would prepare and launch our new titles on Amazon.



Please note that the test trial is paid (fixed payment of $5). You will receive more details via
 if you have been selected to do a test trial. A test trial is not a guarantee of further work.



Payment will be made via PayPal or Wise. (MANDATORY FOR CONSIDERATION)



We review applications on a rolling basis and will contact you to do a paid test trial within 48 hours of your application.

title: Sales Rep
description:
I am looking for motivated, entrepreneurial individuals in the Philippines who are interested in building their own online wellness business through network marketing.



This is not a salaried position. This opportunity is 100% commission-based, meaning your income is based entirely on the sales and team you build.



You will be partnering with LifeWave, a fast-growing wellness company known for its breakthrough light therapy patch technology, including the flagship X39 patch, designed to stimulate the body’s own regenerative processes.



LifeWave’s technology has created a passionate global community and continues to expand internationally. Many people are drawn to this business because it allows them to build income online while promoting innovative wellness technology.



This role is best suited for individuals who want to build a long-term business, not someone looking for a traditional hourly job.



Important: Please Read Before Applying



• This is a network marketing / direct sales opportunity

• This is 100% commission-based (no hourly pay or salary)

• You will be an independent distributor

• There is a required enrollment purchase to start the business (standard distributor enrollment with the company)

• Income depends on your sales, marketing effort, and team building



This opportunity is for individuals who want to build their own business within a proven system.



About the Technology



LifeWave has developed non-transdermal phototherapy patches that work by reflecting specific wavelengths of light back into the body to stimulate biological processes.



The flagship product, X39, is designed to activate a peptide associated with tissue repair and regeneration. Many users report improvements in:



• Energy

• Recovery

• Sleep

• Physical performance

• Overall wellness



The technology is patented and has attracted significant attention in the wellness and biohacking communities.



What You’ll Do



• Promote LifeWave products online

• Share educational content about the technology

• Generate leads through social media and online communities in the Philippines. 

• Sell products directly to customers in the Philippines. 

• Build and mentor your own sales team (optional but encouraged) in the Philippines. 

• Attend optional training calls and tea
etings - all on US time.

title: Content Operations & Creative Performance Virtual Assistant
description:

This is NOT a basic admin role.



This person will be deeply involved in content performance, organization, creative strategy, analytics breakdowns, hiring support, and visual planning.



If you love content, understand why videos go viral (or flop), and enjoy building systems that make creative teams run smoothly — this role is for you.



???? Role Overview



You will act as:



Content Operations Manager



Performance Analyst



Creative Board Builder



Hiring & Talent Research Assistant



Personal Virtual Assistant



You will work directly with the founder and help bring structure, clarity, and performance optimization to our content machine.



???? Key Responsibilities

1?? Content Operations Management



Organize and maintain editor folders and content databases



Keep assets clean, labeled, and updated



Ensure content workflows stay organized and efficient



Do periodic audits to keep everything streamlined



Identify gaps or bottlenecks in our content systems



2?? Content Performance Tracking & Reporting



Track every video posted



Collect and log analytics weekly



Monitor performance trends across platforms



Identify why videos performed well or underperformed



Create weekly performance breakdowns in Canva or Google Slides



Present actionable recommendations (what to fix, what to double down on)



You must understand:



Hooks



Retention



CTR



Thumbnails



Titles



Audience psychology



Storytelling structure



We don’t just want reports — we want insights.



3?? Creative & Visual Planning



Create visual roadmap boards (Miro boards)



Help structure content strategies visually



Build organized idea boards



Help map out content themes and creative direction



Understand what “good content” looks like



You should be visually creative and structured.



4?? Hiring & Talent Research



Source and vet talent (editors, creatives, etc.)



Create a hiring process



Pre-screen candidates



Organize interviews



Help build systems for evaluating talent



We want someone who understands how to identify quality.



5?? Systems & Accountability



Track weekly improvement goals



Make sure action items are completed



Identify weak areas in content performance



Suggest improvements proactively



Hold the team accountable to performance metrics



???? Ideal Candidate



You are:



Extremely organized



Creative and visually sharp



Analytical and data-driven



Proactive (you don’t wait to be told what to fix)



Detail-oriented



A content nerd (you watch and analyze content naturally)



Comfortable using tools like:

Long-term role for the right person

Clean, organized content systems

Strategic recommendations



Improved content metrics over time



Strong hiring pipeline



Smooth operational flow

Experience with content analytics



Tools you are strongest with

Your availability (hours per week)

title: Project Coordinator – Maintenance & Subcontractor Operations
description:
Project Coordinator – Maintenance & Subcontractor Operations



Department:

SJA Pros



Reports To:

Operations Coordinator



Role Type:

Full-Time, Remote



Position Overview

The Project Coordinator plays a critical role in supporting SJA Pros’ maintenance and restoration operations. This position manages large and complex projects—including insurance-related repairs, mitigation follow-through, and multi-trade maintenance scopes—while also supporting the growth of SJA Pros’ subcontractor network through outreach, vetting, and onboarding.

This role ensures that projects move efficiently from initial assessment to completion, that subcontractors meet SJA Pros’ standards for quality and compliance, and that communication between property managers, owners, technicians, and subcontractors remains consistent and timely.



Key Responsibilities

1. Project Coordination – Large Maintenance & Insurance-Related Projects

Serve as the primary coordinator for high-complexity maintenance projects, including insurance claims, mitigation follow-through, flooring replacements, plumbing repairs, multi-trade reconstruction, and vendor-driven scopes.

Review inspection reports, mitigation summaries, scopes of work, and adjuster correspondence to determine next steps and required trades.

Create, assign, and track work orders within ClickUp, maintaining accurate documentation, photos, notes, and schedule updates.

Coordinate with mitigation vendors, adjusters, and specialty trades to ensure timely sequencing of repair phases.

Prepare complete scope packets for subcontractors, including photos, diagrams, itemized task lists, and required materials.

Communicate with owners, property managers, and tenants regarding timelines, access needs, approvals, and project status.

Monitor project progress, follow up on delays, and escalate issues when required.

Verify completion of work, review technician notes and photos, and ensure all tasks are closed out according to SJA Pros standards.

Support invoicing by confirming that completed work matches the approved scope and that all documentation is properly uploaded.



2. Subcontractor Outreach, Vetting & Onboarding

Conduct ongoing outreach to expand SJA Pros’ network of qualified subcontractors across all trades.

Vet potential subcontractors by reviewing licensing, business registration, COIs, specialty licenses (e.g., electrical, plumbing), W-9s, and references.

Request and review pricing sheets, hourly rates, diagnostic fees, and wholesale pricing when applicable.

Ensure all subcontractors meet SJA Pros’ policy requirements—including no location-based trip fees and adherence to Property Meld communication standards.

Onboard approved subcontractors, ensuring they understand SJA Pros’ workflow, expectations, documentation standards, and communication requirements.

Maintain an organized subcontractor database with up-to-date contact information, service areas, specialties, and pricing.

Track subcontractor performance, quality, reliability, and responsiveness to inform future assignment decisions.

Support the Director and Manager in assigning new work opportunities based on performance and capacity.



3. Communication & Coordination Support

Serve as a liaison between Property Managers, tenants, owners, subcontractors, and internal teams.

Draft clear, professional
s to owners and subcontractors regarding repairs, approvals, scheduling, and project updates.

Provide timely follow-ups and document all communication within ClickUp or Property Meld.

Assist with weekly project reviews, internal operations meetings, and coordination updates.



4. Compliance, Documentation & Quality Standards

Ensure all work adheres to SJA Pros standards, Washington State safety requirements, and any specialty trade code compliance.

Maintain meticulous documentation—including photos, moisture logs, inspection notes, and technician updates—for insurance-related projects.

Verify that subcontractors follow safety protocols, professional conduct standards, and SJA Pros’ expectations for tenant interaction.

Support the team in identifying process improvements and updating SOPs to streamline project coordination workflows.





Qualifications

Experience in property management, construction coordination, restoration, or maintenance operations preferred.

Knowledge of insurance claims, mitigation, and multi-trade coordination is a strong plus.

Strong organizational skills with the ability to juggle multiple projects and deadlines.

Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

Ability to build strong relationships with subcontractors and internal teams.

Detail-oriented, process-driven, and comfortable following structured workflows.

Ability to learn quickly, solve problems, and adapt to changing priorities.





Success in This Role Looks Like

Projects run smoothly with minimal delays and complete documentation.

Subcontractors are responsive, reliable, properly vetted, and aligned with SJA Pros’ standards.

Owners and property managers receive timely updates and clear expectations.

Work orders are accurately assigned, tracked, and closed with professional documentation.

Insurance-related jobs include full compliance, detailed photos, and accurate records.

The subcontractor network grows in coverage, quality, and specialization.







HOW TO APPLY

Please send a copy of your resume along with the the following:



1. Self-Introduction Video: Please record a short video (1-2 minutes) introducing yourself, highlighting your relevant experience, and answering the following question: What unique strengths do you feel you would bring to this role? 



2. Typing Test Result: Please complete a typing test and share the results with us. You can use any online typing test platform of your choice.



3. Internet Speed Test Result: Please perform an internet speed test and provide the results, particularly if the interview will be conducted virtually. You can use websites like speedtest.net for this purpose.



We look forward to getting to know you better and discussing how you can contribute to our team.

description:

/escalation process, and continuous improvement

mbers (screening, interviews, onboarding, training)

Requirements (Must-have)

title: Part-Time Email Outreach & Lead Nurture VA (Music Industry)
description:
We help gigging musicians (wedding bands, corporate event musicians, soloists, DJs) book more and better-paid private events through training, community, and a profile platform called 
 where musicians can showcase their act and get discovered.



We already have a working cold
 system (using Instantly) and a growing list of musicians. Our open and reply rates are strong. The bottleneck now is fast, organized, consistent follow-up with people who reply.



That’s where you come in.



Role Overview (Part-Time: 4 hours/day, 5 days/week)



Your main job is to turn replies into results:



- Check our Instantly
 inbox daily

- Tag and organize every reply (interested / later / questions / not a fit)

- Send follow-up
s using templates and your own judgment

- Keep a simple tracking sheet updated (status, last touch, next touch)

- Do basic lead research to add new qualified musician
s to our list



You are not doing high-pressure sales. You are doing smart, polite, consistent lead nurture: fast responses, personalized follow-ups, and clear next steps.



Key Outcomes: (How we’ll measure success)



- 100% of new replies tagged and handled the same day

- Warm leads receive a thoughtful response within 12 hours

- Consistent number of new musician profiles created on 
 each week

- Clean, accurate tracking sheet so no interested lead is forgotten



Daily Tasks:



- Log into Instantly and process all new replies

- Choose & send the correct follow-up template based on lead type

- Lightly personalize messages (using their name, act type, situation)

- Update lead statuses and next follow-up date in our sheet/CRM

- Spend remaining time building new lists of musicians from directories, websites, and Instagram (we’ll give SOPs)



Requirements:



- Strong written English and professional
 tone

- Experience with any of: cold
, appointment setting, lead generation, or customer support

- High attention to detail and reliable daily execution

- Comfortable working with simple tools (Google Sheets,
, basic CRMs)

- Stable internet and ability to work on a consistent daily schedule (PH time)



Nice to Have: (but not required)



- Experience in BPO, sales support, or appointment setting

- Familiarity with Instantly or other cold
 tools

- Interest in music or events (weddings, corporate, live gigs)



How to Apply:



Do NOT send a generic application.



Fill out this short application form: 



In the form, you’ll be asked to:



- Share your experience with lead follow-up / appointment setting

- Write a sample reply to a musician who says, “This looks interesting, but I’m too busy to set up another profile right now.”

- Confirm your available hours (PH time) and desired hourly rate



We’ll start with a paid trial (first 2 weeks) focused on responsiveness, quality of replies, and attention to detail. If it’s a good fit for both of us, this will be a long-term, stable role where you can grow with the business.



Thank you!

title: Senior Video Editor – Direct Response Creative
description:
Senior Video Editor – Direct Response Creative — Remote | $1,100–$1,500/month + Performance Bonuses



Most video editing jobs have you cutting the same corporate talking heads on repeat. Same templates. Same briefs. Same lifeless content that nobody watches.



This is not that job.



We're Whiskey Library — a bourbon subscription brand shipping mystery boxes to 4,000+ members across the U.S. We work with 400+ craft distilleries, and our content has one job: make people stop scrolling, watch, and buy. If your ad doesn't convert, it doesn't matter how pretty it looks.



We're looking for a senior video editor who wants to learn (or already knows) how to create short-form ads that actually drive revenue — not just rack up views.



Why This Role Is Different



You won't be buried under a creative director who rewrites everything. You'll work directly with the founders and our creative strategist. Your edits go live on Meta and TikTok — and you'll see exactly how they perform. When your ad crushes it, you get paid more. Simple as that.



Flexible schedule. We care about output, not clocking hours. Work when you're sharpest, as long as there's overlap with US EST for feedback loops.



Independent work. We give you the brief, the footage, and the framework. You own the edit. No micromanagement, no hovering.



Performance bonuses. When your ad performs well in a direct-response setting (high CTR, strong ROAS), you earn more on top of your base pay. Your best work directly increases your income.



Real growth path. Start as a senior. Prove yourself and move up. Our best editors become creative leads who concept entire campaigns, not just cut footage.



What You'll Be Doing



Editing 15–60 second vertical video ads for Meta 
/Instagram) and TikTok

Selecting the strongest clips from raw UGC footage and building scroll-stopping hooks in the first 3 seconds

Following proven ad structures: Hook ? Problem ? Solution ? CTA

Adding captions, music, sound effects, and transitions that feel native to the platform (not over-produced)

Producing multiple variations of each ad (different hooks, different CTAs) for A/B testing

Taking feedback quickly, iterating same-day, and improving with every round



Who You Are



You've made short-form videos before — even if just for yourself, your friends, or a personal TikTok/Reels account

You understand clip selection and pacing instinctively. You know a boring clip when you see one

You're eager to learn the difference between content that looks good and content that actually sells

You have reliable internet and access to editing software (Premiere Pro, DaVinci Resolve, CapCut Pro, or Final Cut Pro)

You communicate clearly. If you hit a wall, you say so instead of going silent

You can work US EST hours (full-time, 40 hours/week)



Compensation



Base: $1,100–$1,500 USD/month

Performance bonuses tied to ad results. When your creative drives strong metrics, you earn additional pay on top of your base.

Pay increases as you level up. Prove consistent output and quality, and your rate grows.

100% remote, flexible hours (with EST overlap for feedback).



How to Apply



To be considered, you must complete the following:



Fill out our application form and complete the trial task: 



The form includes a short editing assignment. You'll receive access to raw unboxing footage and create 3 short ad variations (15–16 seconds each) using different hooks from the footage provided. This is how we evaluate every candidate.



If you do not submit the form with the completed trial task, your application will not be reviewed.



We review submissions within 24 hours. If you're shortlisted, you'll get feedback and next steps immediately.

/Instagram) and TikTok

Selecting the strongest clips from raw UGC footage and building scroll-stopping hooks in the first 3 seconds

Following proven ad structures: Hook ? Problem ? Solution ? CTA

Adding captions, music, sound effects, and transitions that feel native to the platform (not over-produced)

Producing multiple variations of each ad (different hooks, different CTAs) for A/B testing

Taking feedback quickly, iterating same-day, and improving with every round



Who You Are



You've made short-form videos before — even if just for yourself, your friends, or a personal TikTok/Reels account

You understand clip selection and pacing instinctively. You know a boring clip when you see one

You're eager to learn the difference between content that looks good and content that actually sells

You have reliable internet and access to editing software (Premiere Pro, DaVinci Resolve, CapCut Pro, or Final Cut Pro)

You communicate clearly. If you hit a wall, you say so instead of going silent

You can work US EST hours (full-time, 40 hours/week)



Compensation



Base: $750–$1,000 USD/month

Performance bonuses tied to ad results. When your creative drives strong metrics, you earn additional pay on top of your base.

Pay increases as you level up. Prove consistent output and quality, and your rate grows.

100% remote, flexible hours (with EST overlap for feedback).



How to Apply



To be considered, you must complete the following:



Fill out our application form and complete the trial task: 



The form includes a short editing assignment. You'll receive access to raw unboxing footage and create 3 short ad variations (15–16 seconds each) using different hooks from the footage provided. This is how we evaluate every candidate.



If you do not submit the form with the completed trial task, your application will not be reviewed.



We review submissions within 24 hours. If you're shortlisted, you'll get feedback and next steps immediately.

title: Web Dev & UX / Facebook media buyer & google tag manager / tracking specialist
description:
## Job Title: Web Dev & UX / Meta & Google Media Buyer / Tracking Specialist



### About the Role



We are looking for a versatile and detail-oriented professional who can bridge the gap between web development, user experience, and digital advertising. This role is perfect for someone who understands the technical side of domains, hosting, and tracking, while also having an eye for design and performance-driven media buying.



### Responsibilities



* **Web Development & UX**

  * Ensure websites and landing pages are properly connected, functional, and optimised

  * Build and optimise high-converting landing pages with strong design and UX principles

  * Use Canva (and other tools) to create visually appealing assets that align with brand guidelines for meta ads



* **Digital Advertising**



  * Plan, launch, and manage effective Meta 
/Instagram) ad campaigns

* **Tracking & Analytics**



  * Implement and maintain Google Tag Manager setups

  * Configure accurate tracking for Google Analytics (GA4), Meta Pixel, and other platforms

### Skills & Experience



* Strong understanding of domains, subdomains, hosting, and WordPress (or similar CMS)

* Proven ability to design and optimise landing pages that convert

* Canva design skills with an eye for clean, professional layouts

### Nice to Have

### What We’re Looking For

* Able to balance technical accuracy with creative flair

title: Inside Sales Agent (ISA) – Real Estate
description:
We’re looking for a high-performance Inside Sales Agent (ISA) to join our real estate team. If you have previous experience working with licensed real estate agents and love converting leads into appointments, this role is for you.



The ISA is the heartbeat of our lead generation department. You’ll prospect daily, build strong relationships, nurture incoming and long-term leads, and set appointments that turn into closings.

Convert inquiries into qualified appointments for our listing and buyer specialists

Call past clients for reviews, referrals, and check-ins

Comfortable using scripts and objection handlers

Tech savvy — CRM experience required



Ability to work independently in a fast-paced environment



Preferred

Work Environment

Loves talking to people

Wants to grow within a high-performing real estate team

title: Video Editor (Cantonese Content)
description:
Job Description

About the Role

We are looking for a skilled and detail-oriented Video Editor to join our content team on a freelance/project basis. You will be editing Cantonese-language videos for our brand/channel. Don't worry if you don't speak Cantonese — we will provide full English translation scripts so you can follow the content and edit accurately.

Responsibilities



Edit raw footage into polished, publish-ready videos

Follow provided English translation scripts to understand content flow and pacing

Add subtitles, captions, lower thirds, and on-screen text as directed

Sync audio and video, adjust pacing and cuts for natural flow

Apply colour grading, transitions, and visual effects as needed

Incorporate brand assets (logos, intros/outros, music) per our style guide

Deliver files in specified formats and resolutions (e.g. 1080p, 4K, 9:16, 16:9)

Revise edits based on feedback in a timely manner



Requirements

Proven experience in video editing (portfolio or sample work required)

Proficient in Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, DaVinci Resolve, or CapCut

Ability to work from translation scripts — no Cantonese language skills needed

Strong sense of timing, pacing, and storytelling

Reliable internet connection and ability to handle large video files

Good communication skills in English

Able to meet deadlines consistently



Nice to Have

Experience editing social media content (YouTube, Instagram Reels, TikTok)

Motion graphics or After Effects skills

Experience working with multilingual or dubbed content



What We Provide

Raw footage + full English translation/transcript for every video

Brand style guide and asset kit

Clear brief for each project

Timely feedback and payment

Ledger Lane is a new accounting brand being launched to support businesses outside of the NDIS sector. Our goal is to diversify our services and work with growing businesses across industries such as retail, cafés, hospitality, and professional services.

We are building a high-performing Sales Development team to help launch and scale the business. This role is an opportunity to join as one of the founding tea
mbers and play a key role in shaping the growth strategy of the company.

We are looking for a top-performing Sales Development Representative (SDR) who will help build the foundation of our sales pipeline. This is not a typical SDR role — you will help launch the brand, develop outreach strategies, build lead databases, and contribute to the overall business growth plan.

You will work closely with the leadership team to identify opportunities, create systems, and generate qualified leads for the business.

, LinkedIn, calls, and other channels)

title: Digital Marketing Lead
description:
Role Overview



Own the end-to-end marketing campaigns engine—strategy, creative requests, execution, measurement, and iteration—to generate quality discovery calls for Sales. You’ll lead a two-person pod, coordinate with the Design team for social/video/reels/landing assets, and ensure rigorous attribution and reporting via in-house platform.



What You’ll Do

Strategy & Planning



Design and manage multi-channel campaigns (LinkedIn outreach + ads,
 funnels & nurture, landing pages, partnerships)

title: Marketing Lead
description:
At Yokly, we’re looking for a Marketing Lead who doesn’t just run campaigns — but owns the entire growth engine end to end. This role is for a strategic, data-driven marketer who knows how to turn positioning, funnels, and creative into qualified discovery calls for Sales.



You’ll lead a focused pod, collaborate closely with Design, Sales, and Operations, and run multi-channel campaigns with clear attribution, measurable results, and continuous optimization. If you love building systems, testing ideas, and seeing your work directly influence revenue — this role is built for you.



What You’ll Do

Strategy & Planning

Design and manage multi-channel campaigns: LinkedIn outreach + ads,
 funnels & nurture, landing pages, and partnership campaigns.

Define ICPs, offers, messaging maps, and test hypotheses that shape our go-to-market strategy.



Execution & Systems

Launch and oversee LinkedIn sequences and GoHighLevel automations.

Maintain organized UTMs, campaign taxonomy, and tracking to ensure clean attribution.

Ensure seamless lead handoff into our in-house app/CRM — every lead counts.



Creative Collaboration

Lead creative briefs and SLAs with the Design team (social posts, reels, carousels, landing page blocks).

Review assets to ensure message-market fit, brand consistency, and compliance.

Uphold our brand pillars, style, and voice across all campaigns.



Proposals & Reporting

Build campaign proposals outlining goals, audience, offers, creative briefs, and budgets.

Produce clear performance reports with actionable insights and recommendations for continuous improvement.



Optimization & Learning

Run A/B tests across hooks, offers, landing pages, and sequences.

Capture learnings and build playbooks to elevate future campaigns.



Cross-Team Collaboration

Work closely with Operations, Sales, and Client Success for smooth handoffs, data integrity, and feedback loops.

Be a communicator of wins, challenges, and insights across the team.





???? Who We’re Looking For (Qualifications)

Master’s degree in Marketing, Business Management, or related field.

3+ years in B2B / SaaS / performance marketing roles.

Hands-on experience with LinkedIn outreach,
 funnels, landing pages, and automation (GoHighLevel or similar).

Proven track record running multi-channel campaigns with strong attribution.

Strong skills in brief-writing, creative review, and campaign processes.

Analytical mindset; comfortable working with dashboards, UTMs, and performance data.

Excellent English communication skills.

Collaborative spirit; thrives in fast-paced environments.



Nice to Have

Experience in Fintech, Payments, or SaaS

Deep understanding of funnel optimization and landing-page experimentation

Familiarity with compliance-sensitive marketing



What You Get

Fully remote, full-time role

Weekends off (Saturday & Sunday)

HMO upon regularization

PTO, Sick Leave, Mental Health Leave

PTO conversion to cash

13th Month Pay as our holiday thank-you

title: Healthcare Recruiter
description:
Job description:



We are a growing U.S.-based healthcare company seeking a Recruiter / Interview Specialist to support therapist hiring across multiple departments.



This role is call-heavy and interview-focused, with additional responsibility for coordinating hiring needs and tracking recruitment progress. Approximately 90% of your work will involve phone interviews with therapist candidates.



If you are not comfortable being on calls for most of your shift, this role is not a good fit.



Role Overview



• You will conduct daily phone interviews with licensed therapists (PT, OT, ST, PTA, COTA), coordinate with internal teams on hiring needs, and manage the recruitment pipeline from job posting through candidate handoff.

• You are the first point of contact for candidates and a key link between departments, so professionalism, organization, and communication are essential.



Key Responsibilities



• Interviewing & Screening (Primary Focus)

• Conduct high-volume phone interviews daily with therapist candidates.

• Screen candidates for: Licensure and credentials, Experience and specialties, Availability and geographic coverage,



Communication skills and professionalism

• Clearly explain roles, expectations, and next steps.

• Identify red flags and escalate concerns when necessary.

• Recruitment Coordination & Follow-Ups

• Follow up with candidates after interviews as needed.

• Coordinate with internal departments (staffing, operations, leadership) to understand current and upcoming hiring needs.

• Schedule next steps and hand off qualified candidates appropriately.

• Maintain timely and professional communication with candidates throughout the process.

• Job Posting & Process Tracking

• Post job openings on approved platforms as hiring needs change.

• Track candidates through each stage of the recruitment process.

• Keep recruitment trackers or ATS accurate and up to date.

• Provide basic updates on hiring progress when requested.



What We’re Looking For



• Comfortable being on the phone most of the workday.

• Clear, confident, professional speaking voice.

• Strong listening and judgment skills.

• Highly organized and detail-oriented.

• Able to manage follow-ups and coordination without losing 
ntum.



Qualifications



• 1–2+ years of experience in: Recruiting, Healthcare staffing, HR screening, Call center interviewing

• Excellent spoken and written English.

• Comfortable conducting multiple interviews per day.

• Familiar with Google Workspace (Sheets, Docs).

• Bonus: Experience recruiting healthcare professionals or independent contractors.



Requirements



• Reliable internet connection.

• Quiet, professional work environment for calls.

• Ability to work U.S. (PST) business hours.

• Part-time or full-time availability (to be discussed).



How to apply



• Please send a brief cover letter outlining your experience that is aligned or related to the role you are applying for. Make sure to include the specific tasks you have handled, as well as the link to your resume.

• Kindly monitor your OLJ inbox, as I will send the final interview link and your schedule there if you are shortlisted for the position.





Benefits:



• Paid training

• Work from home



Experience:



• Recruiting: 2 years (Required)

• Healthcare Staffing: 1 year (Required)

• Work Location: Remote

title: VIDEO EDITOR FOR AI & TECH YOUTUBE CHANNEL. FULL-TIME, LONG-TERM POSITION
description:
VIDEO EDITOR FOR AI & TECH YOUTUBE CHANNEL. FULL-TIME, LONG-TERM POSITION



We're a growing YouTube channel covering AI, automation, robotics, and emerging technology. We're hiring 2 dedicated video editors for full-time, long-term positions.



This is not a side gig or one-off freelance project. We're building something real, and we're looking for editors who want to grow with the channel long-term. If you're early in your career, hungry to build a serious portfolio, and ready to commit to consistent, high-volume work, this is your opportunity to get in on the ground floor of a channel that's scaling fast. The editors who prove themselves here will have a clear path to expanded roles, higher pay, and real ownership over production.





WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING



Editing 3 long-form YouTube videos per week (9 to 22 minutes each) plus 1 vertical short (30 to 35 seconds). That's 4 deliverables per week. Each long-form video follows a detailed Editor's Table, a scene-by-scene visual blueprint that tells you exactly what goes on screen at every timecode. Every shot type, every B-roll asset, every visual composition, every documentation link is mapped out for you. You'll never have to guess what the vision is. Your job is to execute it at a high level with creative energy, polished visuals, and dynamic pacing.



Each video requires a visual change every 5 seconds. Zooms, cuts, motion graphics, text animations, transitions, split-screen compositions, kinetic text 
nts, and data overlays. This is fast-paced, high-retention editing designed to keep viewers locked in.



The vertical short is pulled from the best clip across your 3 long-form videos. After probation, this increases to 1 short per video (3 vertical shorts per week).





BREAKING NEWS PRIORITY



We also cover breaking AI news. When a major story drops, you may need to reprioritize your current workload and turn around a breaking news video as fast as possible. This means pausing whatever you're working on and editing the urgent video first. Speed and flexibility matter here. If you can't handle shifting priorities on short notice, this role isn't for you.





WHAT'S PROVIDED VS. WHAT YOU BUILD



Provided by us:



All A-roll footage (AI avatar talking head). You do NOT film or source A-roll.



An Editor's Handbook, your permanent reference guide covering every production standard, shot type definition, B-roll category, branded glitch placement, hook rules, avatar cleanup pipeline, and quality benchmarks. This is your go-to document for how things work on this channel. You'll receive it on day one.



A detailed Editor's Table for every video. A scene-by-scene visual blueprint with exact timecodes, shot types, visual assignments, B-roll tags, Director's Notes, and full spoken dialogue for every scene. You'll never have to guess what the vision is.



Documentation links and screenshot instructions. You capture the screenshots.



Stock footage keywords. You pull the clips from Envato Elements (access included).



After Effects composition directives. You build the compositions from detailed descriptions specifying elements, layout, colors, and animation.



YouTube source video search terms. You source real footage clips using WayinVideo (wayin.ai), a tool that lets you search within YouTube videos to find specific 
nts.



Quote card text and sources. You design and place them.



Full-screen kinetic text lines. You create bold, impactful typography 
nts.



Digital Twin Intro clips. Pre-recorded footage for the channel's signature intro sequence that appears at the start of every video. You'll receive the raw clips and build the intro following the Editor's Table direction.



What you build:



You're not sourcing content or coming up with ideas. Everything is mapped out for you in the Editor's Table, and the Editor's Handbook is your permanent reference for standards. But you ARE building the visuals, compositions, and motion graphics that bring it to life. Your creative judgment matters. How you cut, how you pace, how you build visual energy between scenes is your craft. The Editor's Table is the blueprint. You bring it to life.





THE 40/60 SPLIT



About 40% of each video features the AI avatar on screen. The remaining 60% is full-screen B-roll: custom After Effects compositions, documentation screenshots, source video footage, stock footage, quote cards, and kinetic text. A visual change must happen every 5 seconds. No static frames.





AVATAR SHOT TYPES YOU'LL WORK WITH



You'll work with five shot types that rotate throughout every video:



Full Frame. Pure avatar, direct to camera. Maximum 5 to 6 seconds per clip. Used sparingly at scene endings for emotional payoff.



Split Panel. Avatar on one side (~40% of frame), data/documentation/visual on the other (~60%). This is the primary delivery mode. The panel content changes every 5 seconds.



With Overlay. Avatar visible but kinetic text, stat bars, or lower thirds appear on screen. The viewer's eye is drawn to the data. Maximum 2 per scene.



Zoom Punch. Quick 1 to 2 second tight crop cut on the avatar's face for punchlines and pivots. Target 6 to 8 per video minimum.



PIP Float. Small circular avatar cutout (~30 to 35% of frame) floating over full-screen source footage. Features an orbiting glow trail around the circle edge. Used when real YouTube footage plays underneath the avatar's voice. Target 5 to 7 per video. Can run longer than other shot types (8 to 15 seconds).





B-ROLL TYPES YOU'LL WORK WITH



Every visual in the Editor's Table is tagged with one of six categories:



B.R1, Documentation: Full-screen screenshot of a source page, article, report, or data dashboard. You open the provided link, screenshot the relevant section, and drop it in. This is proof, showing the viewer real evidence.



B.R2, Authority Quote: Quote card overlay attributed to a specific person. For recognizable public figures, include a photo alongside the quote. 0 to 3 per video.



B.R3, AE Composition: Custom After Effects motion graphics. Headline stacks, timelines, comparison charts, data visualizations, ecosystem maps. You build these from detailed written directives. Maximum 8 per video.



B.R4, Stock Footage: Atmospheric footage from Envato Elements. Used for mood and energy, not proof. Short clips only, 3 to 5 seconds maximum.



B.R5, Kinetic Text: Full-screen animated text 
nts for key phrases and thesis statements, or lower third name/title/stat bars. Maximum 12 per video.



B.R6a, Source Video: Real YouTube footage sourced via WayinVideo. The Editor's Table provides search terms. You search YouTube for the title, paste the URL into WayinVideo, use "Find 
nts" with the provided keywords, select the best clip, and trim. Visual only, always mute source audio. Minimum 8 per video. A visible channel credit 
 must appear on screen for every source video clip.





BRANDED GLITCH (SOUND IDENTITY)



Every video features 4 branded glitches, a signature chromatic RGB split with a digital glitch sound effect, lasting 0.3 seconds. These are the ONLY sound effects in the entire video. No additional SFX on stat reveals, transitions, scene changes, or anywhere else. The branded glitch is the sound identity of the channel. Placement is specified in the Editor's Table.





LIVE-ACTION VIDEOS (OCCASIONAL)



The majority of your work will be AI avatar videos. However, there will be occasions where you'll edit videos featuring the real Francis on camera. For example, hands-on product reviews, studio walkthroughs, or special topic deep dives. These videos still follow the same B-roll editing standards (documentation screenshots, source footage, AE compositions, kinetic text) but with real human footage as the A-roll instead of the avatar. The AI cleanup pipeline does not apply to these videos. Frequency will vary, but expect this as part of the role.





AI AVATAR CLEANUP (CRITICAL)



The AI avatar footage contains synthesis artifacts (temporal flickering, face-body boundary distortion, micro-jitter, and compression ghosting) that are visible on large screens and monitors. Before any editing begins, all avatar A-roll must go through this cleanup pipeline:



1. After Effects, Neat Video: Apply to all avatar A-roll. Reduces temporal flickering and compression artifacts. This is the primary tool for cleaning AI avatar footage.



2. After Effects, RSMB (ReelSmart Motion Blur): Add motion blur between frames. Smooths the micro-jitter that causes the "dizzy" feeling on AI faces.



3. After Effects, Mocha Face Tracking: Track the face region, apply subtle skin noise reduction to face only. Do not over-smooth. Keep it natural.



4. After Effects, Face-Body Seam Cleanup: Gaussian blur 0.5 to 1px on the boundary where AI face meets body/neck. Hides the synthesis seam.



5. Premiere Pro, Color Grade: Consistent Lumetri color grade across all avatar shots. Match color temperature with B-roll.



6. Premiere Pro, Film Grain Overlay: Organic film grain at 5 to 10% opacity over entire video. Grain masks digital artifacts and makes footage feel natural.



You must also be able to identify and mask unnatural AI movements, such as awkward hand gestures or slow, robotic eye blinks, through strategic cuts, zooms, B-roll placement, and motion graphics. The goal is to never let the viewer notice something is off.



If you've worked with AI-generated footage before (HeyGen, Synthesia, or similar), that's a big plus. If not, you must be willing to learn these techniques quickly.





REQUIRED SKILLS



These are non-negotiable. If you can't demonstrate these in your portfolio or test project, this role isn't the right fit.



Adobe Premiere Pro. Confident, daily-driver level.



Adobe After Effects. Must be comfortable building custom compositions from written directives (animated diagrams, comparison layouts, data visualizations, framework graphics, headline stacks, timelines).



Split-screen composition. Building and managing dynamic split-panel layouts where content changes every 5 seconds.



Motion graphics and kinetic text. Bold full-screen typography, animated stat callouts, lower thirds, text reveals.



Color grading. Lumetri color correction, skin tone matching, consistent grade across mixed footage sources.



Noise reduction and artifact cleanup. Neat Video or equivalent temporal noise reduction experience.



Face tracking. Mocha or similar tool for selective face region processing.



AI footage awareness. Ability to spot and fix synthesis artifacts (ghosting, jitter, face-body seam, unnatural movement) through cleanup tools and strategic editing choices.





OTHER REQUIREMENTS



A computer capable of handling 4K video editing.

Reliable internet and consistent availability.

Strong attention to detail.

Ability to meet weekly deadlines (3 long-form videos + 1 short per week).

Comfortable using Asana for task management and team communication. All assignments, feedback, and deadlines are managed through Asana.





COMPENSATION AND GROWTH



$1,000 USD per month paid bi-weekly ($500 every two weeks). This is probationary pay for the first 90 days.



The $1,000 monthly rate is based on delivering all 16 videos per month (12 long-form + 4 shorts). Pay is tied to output. If deliverables are consistently missed or incomplete, compensation will be adjusted accordingly.



We cover the Upwork service fee. You receive the full $1,000.



Guaranteed pay increase after 90 days as long as quality remains high and you pass probation.



Full-time, long-term position with room to grow.



Envato Elements access provided for stock footage and assets.



Entry-level candidates and recent graduates welcome. We value reliability and hunger over years of experience.





POST-PROBATION GROWTH (AFTER 90 DAYS)



This is where things get exciting. After probation, top performers take on expanded responsibilities and higher pay. This includes:



YouTube thumbnail creation (templates and direction provided).

Full YouTube publishing: uploading, metadata, thumbnails, scheduling.

Script development and content creation support.

End-to-end video production ownership.

1 short per video (3 vertical shorts per week, up from 1).



Expanded responsibilities come with increased pay. The more you grow, the more you earn. We want editors who see this as a career path, not just a paycheck.





WORKING WITH US. WHAT TO EXPECT



This is a professional working relationship and we want to be upfront about how things work so there are no surprises.



Revisions and Quality Control:

Every video goes through a review process before publishing. You should expect feedback and revision requests on most edits, especially in the first few months as we align on style and standards. Revisions are a normal part of the workflow, not a sign that something went wrong. If something doesn't meet our quality standards, you'll be asked to fix it before it goes live. The goal is to get better together over time.



Confidentiality:

All scripts, workflows, production systems, visual templates, and content strategies you're exposed to through this role are confidential. You may not share, reproduce, or discuss any internal materials, processes, or unreleased content outside of this working relationship. This applies during and after your time with us.



Work Ownership:

All content you create in this role (edits, compositions, motion graphics, templates, shorts, thumbnails) belongs to the channel. You may not reuse, repurpose, or include any work created for us in your personal portfolio or for other clients without written permission.



Availability and Communication:

You're expected to be reachable during agreed working hours via Asana. All assignments, deadlines, feedback, and revisions are managed through Asana, not
, not DMs. When breaking news hits, same-day communication and turnaround may be required. If you're unavailable for an extended period, advance notice is expected.



Publishing Authorization (Post-Probation):

Once you're granted access to the YouTube channel for publishing, you may only upload, schedule, or modify content that has been explicitly approved. No unauthorized uploads, title changes, description edits, or scheduling adjustments without sign-off.





PERFORMANCE STANDARDS AND CONTRACTOR TERMS



This is an independent contractor arrangement through Upwork. This is not an employment relationship and does not include benefits, severance, or employment protections.



During the 90-day probation period, either party may end the arrangement at any time, for any reason, with no notice required.



After probation, we follow a structured feedback process. If quality standards are not being met, deadlines are consistently missed, or communication breaks down, the process is:



1. Verbal feedback identifying the specific issue and what needs to change.

2. Written notice via Asana with a clear improvement timeline.

3. If the issue persists after written notice, the arrangement will be ended.



We are direct about expectations and fair about giving you the chance to improve. That said, certain actions may result in immediate termination without the above process, including but not limited to: breach of confidentiality, unauthorized publishing or modification of channel content, extended unexcused absence without notice, or any conduct that puts the channel's reputation or operations at risk.



We reserve the right to end the arrangement at our discretion. Reasonable notice will be provided whenever possible.



This is designed to be a long-term home. We're not looking to cycle through editors. We want people who grow with us, take ownership, and build something they're proud of. If you bring the work ethic and talent, we'll bring the opportunity, structure, and pay increases to match.





TO APPLY



Link to your portfolio or 2 to 3 sample edits.

Brief description of your editing setup (computer specs, software).

Your availability and timezone.

Short note on why you'd be a good fit.



We're looking for someone reliable, detail-oriented, and excited to grow with a channel focused on cutting-edge AI content. This is a full-time commitment, ideal for someone early in their career looking to grow with the channel and business

title: Admin / Executive Assistant
description:
Organisation: Ohana Community Services (Queensland, Australia)

Location: Remote

Reports To: Chief Executive Officer (CEO)

Employment Type: Full-Time 



Position Summary



The Administrative Officer / Executive Assistant to the CEO plays a critical role in supporting the operational efficiency and strategic direction of Ohana Community Services. This position provides high-level executive support to the CEO while also managing key administrative and financial processes across the organisation.



The role requires strong organisational skills, financial administration experience, and proficiency in Xero accounting software, particularly in payroll processing, bank reconciliations, and invoicing. The successful candidate will act as a trusted support to the CEO, ensuring day-to-day operations run smoothly while maintaining accurate financial and administrative records.



This role requires a high degree of professionalism, discretion, and the ability to manage competing priorities in a fast-paced and mission-driven environment.



Key Responsibilities

* Executive Support

- Provide high-level administrative support to the CEO, including calendar management, meeting coordination, and travel arrangements.

title: Canadian P&C Insurance Quoting Specialist
description:
About Us:

We are a tech-enabled offshore staffing agency providing premium back-office support strictly to Canadian Property & Casualty (P&C) insurance brokerages. We equip our team with proprietary "Metaone" workflow automation, making your job smoother, faster, and more efficient. We are looking for an elite Quoting Support Specialist who deeply understands the Canadian insurance market to help our licensed brokers close more deals.



About the Role:

This is NOT a sales role, and there is ZERO cold calling. You will strictly operate as behind-the-scenes administrative support, ensuring full compliance with Canadian provincial licensing regulations.



Your primary job is to act as a "time multiplier" for our Canadian brokers. When a broker collects a client's information, you will take that raw data and accurately input it into Canadian rating engines. You will generate a clean, accurate pricing spreadsheet and send it back to the broker so they can make the sale.



Key Responsibilities:



Receive raw client data (Canadian Driver's License numbers, 17-digit VINs, Postal Codes, home square footage, claims history) from local brokers.



Perform rapid, highly accurate data entry into Canadian P&C rating/quoting software (specifically CompuQuote, Applied Rating Services / ARS, or EZLynx Canada).



Run quotes across 10+ different Canadian insurance carriers (e.g., Intact, Aviva, Wawanesa, Economical, Definity, RSA) to find the most competitive rates.



Perform Quality Assurance (QA) checks to ensure all province-specific and eligible discounts are applied (e.g., Winter Tires, Multi-Line, Retiree discounts, Commute distance).



Compile the results into a standardized Excel/Google Sheets pricing comparison and return it to the broker rapidly.



What We Are Looking For (Requirements):



Strict Experience Requirement: Minimum 1 to 2+ years working as a Rater, Quoting Assistant, or Data Entry Specialist specifically for a Canadian P&C insurance brokerage. (Note: US insurance experience does not qualify for this role due to completely different laws, postal code systems, and software).



Software Knowledge: Proven hands-on experience using Canadian comparative rating software (CompuQuote or Applied Rating Services is a must). Familiarity with Broker Management Systems like Applied EPIC or PowerBroker is a major plus.



Market Knowledge: Familiarity with Canadian auto and home policies and rating factors (understanding how Postal Codes and provincial driving records affect rates, and familiarity with Autoplus reports).



Speed & Accuracy: 50+ WPM typing speed with near 100?curacy. You must be able to input complex data like VINs without making a single mistake.

title: AI Agent Developer
description:
Hi! I'm looking for someone to help build an AI research agent for writers.



The goal is to create a tool that helps writers quickly research topics for long-form YouTube scripts.



For example, a writer could enter a topic like “Dark Souls 1 facts”. The AI would then automatically research the topic across the internet and gather useful information.



The system should be able to:



• Search across multiple sources (websites, wikis, forums, Reddit, old
 posts, YouTube transcripts, etc.)

• Collect facts, details, and interesting information about the topic

• Remove duplicates and filter out low-quality or unreliable information

• Prioritize accurate and trustworthy sources

• Rank facts by how interesting, unique, or obscure they are



The output should be a structured list of facts, something like:



* Fact

* Source link

* Confidence / reliability

* Why it’s interesting or useful



This will allow writers to quickly see the most interesting facts first, then use them while writing scripts.



The goal is to speed up research and help discover unique details that writers might otherwise miss.



Ideally the system should work automatically:



1. Topic is entered

2. AI gathers information

3. Results are organized and ranked



If you've built AI agents, research tools, or similar systems before, I’d love to hear about your experience.

Thank you!

title: Canadian P&C Quoting Assistant (CompuQuote/ARS Expert) - 100% Non-Voice
description:
TYPE OF WORK: Full Time (40 Hours/Week)

WAGE / SALARY: $5.60 – $7.50 USD per hour

HOURS: Night Shift (EST Timezone)



JOB OVERVIEW



About Us:

Step away from the phones! We are a modern, tech-forward offshore staffing agency that partners exclusively with Canadian Property & Casualty (P&C) insurance brokerages. By combining top-tier offshore talent with our proprietary "Metaone" automation tech, we make the quoting process faster and less stressful. We are looking for a Senior Quoting Specialist to handle the heavy data entry so our Canadian brokers can focus on closing deals.



About the Role:

Let’s be clear: This is a 100% non-voice, pure back-office role. You will never have to make a cold call or deal with an angry customer.



As a Senior Quoting Assistant, you are the engine behind the sales team. Local Canadian brokers will send you raw client data. Your job is to rapidly input that data into Canadian rating engines, compare prices across multiple carriers, and send a polished pricing spreadsheet back to the broker. Accuracy and speed are your best tools.



What You Will Do (Key Responsibilities):



Data Processing: Receive broker notes containing sensitive client details (Canadian Driver’s License numbers, Autoplus claims history, Postal Codes, and 17-digit VINs).



System Rating: Perform high-speed, flawless data entry directly into Canadian quoting software (CompuQuote, Applied Rating Services, or EZLynx Canada).



Market Comparison: Run simultaneous quotes across top Canadian insurance markets (such as Intact, Aviva, Wawanesa, Economical, and Definity).



Discount Verification: Act as the final Quality Assurance check to ensure all Canadian-specific discounts are applied correctly (e.g., Winter Tires, Retiree, Multi-Line, and Telematics discounts).



Reporting: Compile the top premium results into an easy-to-read Excel/Google Sheets breakdown for the broker.



What You Must Have (Requirements):



The Golden Rule: You MUST have 1 to 2+ years of hands-on experience quoting for a Canadian P&C brokerage. (Please note: The US and Canadian insurance systems are entirely different. Candidates with only US P&C experience will not be considered).



Software Mastery: You must be highly proficient in CompuQuote or Applied Rating Services (ARS). Experience with Broker Management Systems like Applied EPIC is a massive advantage.



High-Speed Accuracy: A typing speed of 50+ WPM with near 100?curacy. One wrong number on a VIN changes the whole quote!



Canadian Market Knowledge: You understand how Postal Codes affect territory rating and know your way around standard Canadian auto and home coverages.

title: Duda Dynamic Pages Setup + 1 Dynamic Template (Lead Generation Website)
description:
Project Description

I am building a lead generation / rank-and-rent website for service contractors using Duda.

Examples of contractor niches this structure will support:

mold remediation





pool contractors





concrete contractors





foundation repair





roofing





other home service contractors





This project is Phase 1 of a larger project.

If the work is done well, I will immediately hire you for Phase 2 to complete the rest of the templates and demo pages.



Important: This Project Uses Duda Dynamic Pages

This project must use:

Duda Dynamic Pages / Collections

Please do not build static pages only.

The goal is to create a dynamic template system that can later scale to many pages.



Phase 1 Scope of Work

This phase is intentionally small.

You only need to complete two tasks.



Task 1 — Create a Duda Collection

Create a dynamic collection (database) in Duda.

Example fields may include:

city





state





service





problem





option_a





option_b





section_heading





section_content





Add 3–5 sample rows so the dynamic template can display content.

Placeholder data is fine.



Task 2 — Create ONE Dynamic Page Template

Create one dynamic page template connected to the collection.

Template type:

Service Page Template

Suggested structure:

Section 1 — Hero

 Section 2 — Intro

 Section 3 — Service Process

 Section 4 — Local Expertise

 Section 5 — CTA

Each section should include:

heading





subheading





paragraph text





optional button





Use dynamic fields where possible.

Example:

{{service}} in {{city}}, {{state}}



Design Direction

This is a lead generation website for contractor services.

The design should be:

simple





clean





conversion-focused





professional





mobile responsive





This is not a complex design project.

Focus on:

clean layout





clear CTAs





usability







Phase 1 Deliverables

Please provide:

• Duda collection created

 • sample rows added

 • 1 dynamic page template connected to the collection

 • responsive layout

 • clean structure



What Is NOT Required

To keep Phase 1 simple:

You do not need to provide:

final copywriting





SEO optimization





advanced design





animations





integrations







Timeline

Phase 1 should take about:

1 day



Important (Application Instructions)

Please start your proposal with:

DYNAMIC DUDA

Then answer these three questions:

Have you built Duda Dynamic Pages using Collections before?

What would be your first step when creating a dynamic template structure in Duda?

Please include one screenshot of a Duda project you have worked on.







If Phase 1 Goes Well

If the work is good, I will immediately continue with Phase 2.

Phase 2 will include building the remaining templates and demo website.
